title: 64-Byte Transaction Bug
description: A 64-byte witness-stripped transaction can look like the preimage of an internal Merkle-tree node, creating ambiguity that can be abused against SPV proofs and other partial block verifiers. Proposed fixes such as BIP 53 and the consensus-cleanup package remove or constrain that edge case rather than relying on every verifier to defend against it separately.

resources.title: BIP 53: Disallow 64-byte Transactions
resources.notes: Primary proposal describing the Merkle ambiguity and SPV-proof attack surface created by 64-byte witness-stripped transactions.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Merkle Tree Vulnerabilities
resources.notes: Canonical overview of why internal-node ambiguity matters and how 64-byte transactions fit into the mitigation debate.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Consensus Cleanup Soft Fork
resources.notes: Tracks the broader cleanup proposal that includes invalidating future 64-byte transactions.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad reference for transactions, block commitments, and SPV assumptions needed to understand the bug.
